title: AWS S3 Access Denied Errors After Bucket Policy Update
symptoms: S3 GetObject calls returning 403 AccessDenied for application IAM role
Website loading without CSS, JavaScript, or images
CloudTrail logs showing denied S3 API calls from VPC endpoint
S3 bucket policy recently modified removing VPC endpoint condition
Direct S3 access via console working (IAM admin role unaffected)
root_cause: S3 bucket policy update intended to block public access also removed the VPC endpoint policy statement that allowed application server access via the VPC endpoint
resolution: Added VPC endpoint policy statement back to the S3 bucket policy while maintaining the public access restrictions, verified asset loading restored across all services
preventive_actions: Implement S3 bucket policy change simulation before applying
Create bucket policy templates that include standard VPC endpoint access
Add S3 access monitoring with immediate alerting on AccessDenied spikes
Implement bucket policy change approval workflow
Create pre-deployment checklist for S3 policy modifications

timeline.event: S3 bucket policy updated to restrict public access
Application servers begin receiving AccessDenied on asset requests
Website starts loading without CSS, JS, and images
Alert fires for asset loading failures
Lisa Park investigates, identifies bucket policy change
Missing VPC endpoint policy statement identified
VPC endpoint statement added back to bucket policy
Asset loading restored, website rendering normally

embedding_summary: S3 bucket returning AccessDenied after bucket policy update removed VPC endpoint access statement while blocking public access. All static assets unavailable for 20 minutes affecting 18,000 users. Website rendered without styling. Resolved by restoring VPC endpoint policy while maintaining public access restrictions.
